passage.title: Learning English for a Hotel Job

paragraphs.text: Many people want to work in the hotel industry because it is exciting and offers good careers. To get a hotel job, you need good English skills. However, you do not need perfect grammar. In fact, most hotel managers prefer staff who are polite, friendly, and ready to help guests. In addition, many hotels provide free on-the-job training, where new workers learn the standard rules and phrases. This training helps them become more independent [R] in their roles.

In a hotel, listening and speaking are the most important communication skills. Receptionists and other staff must talk to customers every day. Because guests come from many different countries, they often speak with different accents and at different speeds. Having a good ear helps workers understand reservations, check-ins, and the daily schedule [R] of the hotel. If staff listen carefully, they can avoid serious mistakes.

Fortunately, you can prepare for a hotel job in several simple ways. First, you can visit popular hotel websites and write down useful vocabulary. Second, watching educational videos about hotel English online is very helpful. Finally, you can use a digital planner [R] or app to manage your study time and practice listening to various accents by watching international travel programs. These steps will make you feel more confident when you apply for a job.

section_2_detailed: confirm (verb) /kənˈfɜːm/ : to state that something is definitely true or correct - xác nhận. Ví dụ: Please confirm your hotel booking by email.
polite (adjective) /pəˈlaɪt/ : having good manners and respect for others - lịch sự. Ví dụ: The hotel receptionist was very polite and helpful.
available (adjective) /əˈveɪ.lə.bəl/ : free to be used or bought - có sẵn, trống. Ví dụ: Are there any single rooms available for tonight?
staff (noun) /stɑːf/ : the group of people who work for an organization - nhân viên. Ví dụ: The hotel staff are trained to assist guests.
customer (noun) /ˈkʌs.tə.mər/ : a person who buys goods or services - khách hàng. Ví dụ: The customer asked for a quiet room.
book (verb) /bʊk/ : to reserve a room, ticket, or table in advance - đặt trước. Ví dụ: You should book your room online to save money.
cancel (verb) /ˈkæn.səl/ : to decide that a reservation will not happen - hủy. Ví dụ: He had to cancel his trip due to bad weather.
receptionist (noun) /rɪˈsep.ʃən.ɪst/ : a person who works at the front desk of a hotel - nhân viên lễ tân. Ví dụ: The receptionist gave us our room keys.
guesthouse (noun) /ˈɡest.haʊs/ : a small private hotel where guests pay to stay - nhà khách, nhà nghỉ. Ví dụ: Staying at a local guesthouse is cheaper than a hotel.
deposit (noun) /dɪˈpɒz.ɪt/ : a sum of money paid as a first payment - tiền đặt cọc. Ví dụ: The hotel requires a fifty-dollar deposit.
reservation (noun) /ˌrez.əˈveɪ.ʃən/ : an arrangement to keep a room or seat - sự đặt chỗ trước. Ví dụ: I would like to make a reservation for next weekend.
accommodation (noun) /əˌkɒm.əˈdeɪ.ʃən/ : a place to stay, such as a hotel room - chỗ ở. Ví dụ: The city offers many kinds of accommodation for tourists.
check-in (noun phrase) /tʃek ɪn/ : the process of arriving and registering at a hotel - thủ tục nhận phòng. Ví dụ: The standard check-in time is 2:00 PM.
check-out (noun phrase) /tʃek aʊt/ : the process of paying and leaving a hotel - thủ tục trả phòng. Ví dụ: Please complete your check-out before noon.
room service (noun phrase) /ruːm ˈsɜː.vɪs/ : food and drinks delivered to a hotel room - dịch vụ ăn uống tại phòng. Ví dụ: We ordered breakfast through room service.

business facility (noun phrase) /ˈbɪz.nɪs fəˈsɪl.ə.ti/ : amenities provided for work, like Wi-Fi or printers - tiện ích văn phòng, kinh doanh. Ví dụ: The hotel has a business facility with free internet.
make a request (verb phrase) /meɪk ə rɪˈkwest/ : to ask for something politely - đưa ra yêu cầu. Ví dụ: You can make a request for extra pillows.

file a complaint (verb phrase) /faɪl ə kəmˈpleɪnt/ : to make an official statement that something is wrong - nộp đơn khiếu nại, phản ánh. Ví dụ: The guest decided to file a complaint about the noisy AC.
on-the-job training (noun phrase) /ɒn ðə dʒɒb ˈtreɪ.nɪŋ/ : learning skills while doing a job - đào tạo tại chỗ. Ví dụ: The hotel provides free on-the-job training.
good ear (noun phrase) /ɡʊd ɪər/ : the ability to listen well and recognize sounds/accents - khả năng nghe tốt. Ví dụ: Having a good ear helps you understand accents.

targets.name: Past Simple
targets.reason: Necessary for narrating past experiences and actions in hotel context.

targets.name: Modals
targets.reason: Necessary for stating obligations, advice, and capabilities in a hotel environment.

guide.heading: #### Past Simple
guide.content: We use the past simple to describe completed actions in the past. Regular verbs end in -ed (e.g. booked, stayed). Irregular verbs change form (e.g. spoke, had, was/were).

guide.heading: #### Modals of Advice and Obligation
guide.content:
- **must** / **have to**: Strong obligation or rules (e.g. You must show passport).
- **should**: Advice or recommendations (e.g. You should book early).
- **can** / **cannot**: Ability or permission (e.g. You can call front desk, You cannot smoke).

common_mistakes.why_it_matters: Modals must be followed directly by a bare infinitive.

common_mistakes.trap: Using present tense in past contexts
common_mistakes.why_it_matters: Past actions require past simple verbs.

1. want / a / hotel / I / book / to / room .

3. hotel / near / is / station / the / the .

success_criteria: Place words in the correct grammatical order.

tasks.prompt:
Kết hợp hai câu đơn sau đây thành một câu phức sử dụng từ trong ngoặc. Sử dụng dấu gạch ngang thay cho danh sách số:
- The hotel room is small. It is very clean. (but)
- I want to check in early. I am very tired. (because)
- We booked a guesthouse. The hotel was full. (because)
success_criteria: Maintain correct sentence structure and meaning.

tasks.prompt:
Đọc thông tin của du khách sau đây và hoàn thành mẫu đăng ký phòng khách sạn bên dưới:

I would like to book a double room for next weekend. We plan to arrive on Friday and stay for two nights. Is there a room available? Thank you.
Best regards,
John

topic: Booking hotels and simple travel arrangements
practice_markdown:
Reading: A2 Writing: A2 ## Reading Source Verification | Source title | Publisher/Organization | Date/Access date | Final verified URL | URL status | Source type | Reliability check | How the passage uses the source | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | You Can Learn English for a Hotel Job | VOA Learning English | 2019-06-22 / 2026-07-10 | https://www.voanews.com/a/4969246.html | 200 OK | Educational News Article | VOA is an official and extremely reputable English learning provider. | Directly explains the vocabulary and language needed for hotel careers. | ## Warm-up Answer the following questions in English: 1. Do you like staying at hotels or guesthouses when you travel? 2. What do you think is the most important job in a hotel? 3. Why is it important for hotel workers to speak English? ## Reading Passage ### Learning English for a Hotel Job Many people want to work in the hotel industry because it is exciting and offers good careers. To get a hotel job, you need good English skills. However, you do not need perfect grammar. In fact, most hotel managers prefer staff who are polite, friendly, and ready to help guests. In addition, many hotels provide free on-the-job training, where new workers learn the standard rules and phrases. This training helps them become more independent [R] in their roles. In a hotel, listening and speaking are the most important communication skills. Receptionists and other staff must talk to customers every day. Because guests come from many different countries, they often speak with different accents and at different speeds. Having a good ear helps workers understand reservations, check-ins, and the daily schedule [R] of the hotel. If staff listen carefully, they can avoid serious mistakes. Fortunately, you can prepare for a hotel job in several simple ways. First, you can visit popular hotel websites and write down useful vocabulary. Second, watching educational videos about hotel English online is very helpful. Finally, you can use a digital planner [R] or app to manage your study time and practice listening to various accents by watching international travel programs. These steps will make you feel more confident when you apply for a job. ## Questions for Reading ### Questions 1–5: True/False/Not Given 1. Educational videos online C. Social media chat groups D. Online dictionary databases 11. How can watching international travel programs help candidates prepare for hotel jobs? A. To suggest practical ways to prepare for a hotel job D. To compare hotels and guesthouses around the world 13. What is the overall message of the passage? A. You must speak perfect English with no accent to get a hotel job. B. Preparing vocabulary and listening skills can help you get a hotel job. C. Hotels are the only places where you can improve your English. D. Guests from other countries do not care about polite communication. ## Questions for Grammar ### multiple choice 1. Yesterday, the receptionist ________ my booking details. A. confirms B. confirmed C. confirming D. was confirm 2. We ________ a room at the Cozy Homestay last night. A. book B. booked C. booking D. were book 3. The manager ________ to the customer about the noise. A. speak B. spoke C. spoken D. speaking 4. They ________ their hotel reservation two days ago. A. canceled B. cancel C. canceling D. cancels 5. She ________ at the front desk when the guest arrived. A. is B. was C. were D. be 6. The hotel ________ very busy during the holiday weekend. A. was B. is C. were D. been 7. Last summer, they ________ at a small guesthouse in the countryside. A. stay B. stayed C. staying D. stays 8. I ________ to pay a deposit for the room last week. You ________ book your hotel room early because it is the busy season. A. should B. must to C. could to D. have 13. All staff ________ be polite and friendly to the guests. It is required by the manager. A. can B. have to C. could D. should to 14. Guests ________ use the swimming pool after 10 PM. It is closed. A. cannot B. don't have to C. shouldn't to D. couldn't 15. Correct the error: Yesterday, I cancel my hotel room because I could not travel. 24. Correct the error: You should not to make noise in the lobby late at night. 25. (have to) 27. Rewrite the sentence using the word in brackets: I recommend that you stay at this guesthouse. (should) 28. Rewrite the sentence using the word in brackets: We are not allowed to smoke in the rooms. (must not) 29. ## Vocabulary Table
| Từ/Cụm từ | Phiên âm | Loại từ | Định nghĩa và Tiếng Việt | Ví dụ minh họa |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| confirm | /kənˈfɜːm/ | verb | xác nhận (to state that something is definitely true or correct) | Please confirm your hotel booking by email. |
| polite | /pəˈlaɪt/ | adjective | lịch sự (having good manners and respect for others) | The hotel receptionist was very polite and helpful. |
| available | /əˈveɪ.lə.bəl/ | adjective | có sẵn, trống (free to be used or bought) | Are there any single rooms available for tonight? |
| staff | /stɑːf/ | noun | nhân viên (the group of people who work for an organization) | The hotel staff are trained to assist guests. |
| customer | /ˈkʌs.tə.mər/ | noun | khách hàng (a person who buys goods or services) | The customer asked for a quiet room. |
| book | /bʊk/ | verb | đặt trước (to reserve a room, ticket, or table in advance) | You should book your room online to save money. |
| cancel | /ˈkæn.səl/ | verb | hủy (to decide that a reservation will not happen) | He had to cancel his trip due to bad weather. |
| receptionist | /rɪˈsep.ʃən.ɪst/ | noun | nhân viên lễ tân (a person who works at the front desk of a hotel) | The receptionist gave us our room keys. |
| guesthouse | /ˈɡest.haʊs/ | noun | nhà khách, nhà nghỉ (a small private hotel where guests pay to stay) | Staying at a local guesthouse is cheaper than a hotel. |
| deposit | /dɪˈpɒz.ɪt/ | noun | tiền đặt cọc (a sum of money paid as a first payment) | The hotel requires a fifty-dollar deposit. |
| reservation | /ˌrez.əˈveɪ.ʃən/ | noun | sự đặt chỗ trước (an arrangement to keep a room or seat) | I would like to make a reservation for next weekend. |
| accommodation | /əˌkɒm.əˈdeɪ.ʃən/ | noun | chỗ ở (a place to stay, such as a hotel room) | The city offers many kinds of accommodation for tourists. |
| check-in | /tʃek ɪn/ | noun phrase | thủ tục nhận phòng (the process of arriving and registering at a hotel) | The standard check-in time is 2:00 PM. |
| check-out | /tʃek aʊt/ | noun phrase | thủ tục trả phòng (the process of paying and leaving a hotel) | Please complete your check-out before noon. |
| room service | /ruːm ˈsɜː.vɪs/ | noun phrase | dịch vụ ăn uống tại phòng (food and drinks delivered to a hotel room) | We ordered breakfast through room service. |
| business facility | /ˈbɪz.nɪs fəˈsɪl.ə.ti/ | noun phrase | tiện ích văn phòng, kinh doanh (amenities provided for work, like Wi-Fi or printers) | The hotel has a business facility with free internet. |
| make a request | /meɪk ə rɪˈkwest/ | verb phrase | đưa ra yêu cầu (to ask for something politely) | You can make a request for extra pillows. |
| file a complaint | /faɪl ə kəmˈpleɪnt/ | verb phrase | nộp đơn khiếu nại, phản ánh (to make an official statement that something is wrong) | The guest decided to file a complaint about the noisy AC. |
| on-the-job training | /ɒn ðə dʒɒb ˈtreɪ.nɪŋ/ | noun phrase | đào tạo tại chỗ (learning skills while doing a job) | The hotel provides free on-the-job training. |
| good ear | /ɡʊd ɪər/ | noun phrase | khả năng nghe tốt (the ability to listen well and recognize sounds/accents) | Having a good ear helps you understand accents. |

## Recycled Vocabulary (Từ vựng ôn tập)
| Từ/Cụm từ | Phiên âm | Loại từ | Định nghĩa và Tiếng Việt | Bài học gốc (Day) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| independent | /ˌɪn.dɪˈpen.dənt/ | adjective | độc lập, tự chủ | Day 20260705 |
| schedule | /ˈʃedʒ.uːl/ | noun | lịch trình | Day 20260705 |
| planner | /ˈplæn.ər/ | noun | sổ kế hoạch, công cụ lên lịch | Day 20260705 |

## Detailed Grammar Guide
#### Past Simple
We use the past simple to describe completed actions in the past. Regular verbs end in -ed (e.g. booked, stayed). Irregular verbs change form (e.g. spoke, had, was/were).

#### Modals of Advice and Obligation
- **must** / **have to**: Strong obligation or rules (e.g. You must show passport).
- **should**: Advice or recommendations (e.g. You should book early).
- **can** / **cannot**: Ability or permission (e.g. You can call front desk, You cannot smoke).

## Common Mistakes / IELTS Traps for This Topic
| Common mistake / Trap | Wrong example | Correct version | Why it matters for IELTS |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Adding 'to' after 'must' or 'should' | Guests must to pay now. | Guests must pay now. | Modals must be followed directly by a bare infinitive. |
| Using present tense in past contexts | Yesterday, we check in late. | Yesterday, we checked in late. | Past actions require past simple verbs. |

## Writing Guidance / Suggested Answers

#### Task 1
> **Suggested Model Answer:**
> "1. I want to book a hotel room.
2. Is breakfast included in the price?
3. The hotel is near the station."

- **Hướng dẫn viết từng câu / từng bước:** Sắp xếp chủ ngữ đứng trước, sau đó là động từ chính và tân ngữ. Đối với câu hỏi, đưa động từ 'Is' lên đầu câu.
- *Tự kiểm tra:* * Tôi đã viết đúng thứ tự từ trong cả 3 câu chưa? * Tôi đã viết hoa chữ cái đầu và có dấu chấm/hỏi cuối câu chưa?

#### Task 2
> **Suggested Model Answer:**
> "- The hotel room is small but it is very clean.
- I want to check in early because I am very tired.
- We booked a guesthouse because the hotel was full."

- **Hướng dẫn viết từng câu / từng bước:** Liên từ 'but' dùng để nối hai vế tương phản. Liên từ 'because' chỉ nguyên nhân.
- *Tự kiểm tra:* * Tôi đã ghép đúng 2 câu đơn thành 1 câu chưa? * Tôi đã dùng đúng 'but' và 'because' cho từng trường hợp chưa?

#### Task 3
**Suggested Model Answer:**
| Registration Field | Guest Information |
| --- | --- |
| Guest Name | John Smith |
| Country | Canada |
| Arrival Date | 12th October |
| Departure Date | 15th October |
| Room Type | Single room |

- **Hướng dẫn viết từng câu / từng bước:** Điền thông tin chính xác từ phần mô tả. Tránh viết sai chính tả tên riêng 'John Smith' và quốc gia 'Canada'.
- *Tự kiểm tra:* * Tôi đã điền đầy đủ cả 5 trường thông tin chưa? * Tên và ngày tháng đã viết đúng chính tả chưa?

#### Task 4
> **Suggested Model Answer:**
> "Cozy Homestay is located 200 meters from the beach. It also has a swimming pool for guests. The price per night is forty dollars."

- **Hướng dẫn viết từng câu / từng bước:** Viết 3 câu đơn giản mô tả 3 đặc điểm: khoảng cách ra biển (200m), bể bơi (có), và giá cả ($40). Sử dụng cấu trúc 'It has...' và 'The price is...'.
- *Tự kiểm tra:* * Tôi đã viết chính xác 3 câu chưa? * Tôi đã sử dụng thông tin từ bảng Cozy Homestay chưa?

#### Task 5
> **Suggested Model Answer:**
> "Dear Hotel Manager,
I would like to book a double room for next weekend. We plan to arrive on Friday and stay for two nights. Is there a room available? Thank you.
Best regards,
John"

- **Hướng dẫn viết từng câu / từng bước:** Email cần có lời chào lịch sự đầu thư, nêu rõ yêu cầu đặt phòng đôi và thời gian (next weekend), hỏi về tình trạng phòng trống và kết thư bằng lời chúc.
- *Tự kiểm tra:* * Email của tôi đã có đủ phần mở đầu và kết thúc chưa? * Tôi đã đề cập đến phòng đôi và cuối tuần tới chưa? * Độ dài thư có nằm trong khoảng 4-5 câu không?

## Review Bridge
#### IV. Review Bridge / Ôn tập liên chủ đề
1. The weather in summer is ________ than in winter. (A. hot, B. hotter, C. hottest, D. more hot)
- **Đáp án:** B. hotter
- **Giải thích:** Sử dụng tính từ ngắn ở dạng so sánh hơn 'hotter' vì có từ 'than' đứng sau.

2. Today is the ________ day of the year. (A. cold, B. colder, C. coldest, D. most cold)
- **Đáp án:** C. coldest
- **Giải thích:** Sử dụng tính từ ngắn ở dạng so sánh nhất 'coldest' vì có mạo từ 'the' đứng trước.

3. We decided to stay inside ________ it was raining heavily. (A. but, B. so, C. because, D. although)
- **Đáp án:** C. because
- **Giải thích:** Liên từ 'because' chỉ nguyên nhân (bởi vì trời mưa to nên quyết định ở trong nhà).

## Section 2: Detailed Vocabulary List (Học từ vựng đầy đủ)
| Từ vựng + IPA + Loại từ | Nghĩa tiếng Việt |
| --- | --- |
| confirm (verb) /kənˈfɜːm/ | to state that something is definitely true or correct - xác nhận. Ví dụ: Please confirm your hotel booking by email. |
| polite (adjective) /pəˈlaɪt/ | having good manners and respect for others - lịch sự. Ví dụ: The hotel receptionist was very polite and helpful. |
| available (adjective) /əˈveɪ.lə.bəl/ | free to be used or bought - có sẵn, trống. Ví dụ: Are there any single rooms available for tonight? |
| staff (noun) /stɑːf/ | the group of people who work for an organization - nhân viên. Ví dụ: The hotel staff are trained to assist guests. |
| customer (noun) /ˈkʌs.tə.mər/ | a person who buys goods or services - khách hàng. Ví dụ: The customer asked for a quiet room. |
| book (verb) /bʊk/ | to reserve a room, ticket, or table in advance - đặt trước. Ví dụ: You should book your room online to save money. |
| cancel (verb) /ˈkæn.səl/ | to decide that a reservation will not happen - hủy. Ví dụ: He had to cancel his trip due to bad weather. |
| receptionist (noun) /rɪˈsep.ʃən.ɪst/ | a person who works at the front desk of a hotel - nhân viên lễ tân. Ví dụ: The receptionist gave us our room keys. |
| guesthouse (noun) /ˈɡest.haʊs/ | a small private hotel where guests pay to stay - nhà khách, nhà nghỉ. Ví dụ: Staying at a local guesthouse is cheaper than a hotel. |
| deposit (noun) /dɪˈpɒz.ɪt/ | a sum of money paid as a first payment - tiền đặt cọc. Ví dụ: The hotel requires a fifty-dollar deposit. |
| reservation (noun) /ˌrez.əˈveɪ.ʃən/ | an arrangement to keep a room or seat - sự đặt chỗ trước. Ví dụ: I would like to make a reservation for next weekend. |
| accommodation (noun) /əˌkɒm.əˈdeɪ.ʃən/ | a place to stay, such as a hotel room - chỗ ở. Ví dụ: The city offers many kinds of accommodation for tourists. |
| check-in (noun phrase) /tʃek ɪn/ | the process of arriving and registering at a hotel - thủ tục nhận phòng. Ví dụ: The standard check-in time is 2:00 PM. |
| check-out (noun phrase) /tʃek aʊt/ | the process of paying and leaving a hotel - thủ tục trả phòng. Ví dụ: Please complete your check-out before noon. |
| room service (noun phrase) /ruːm ˈsɜː.vɪs/ | food and drinks delivered to a hotel room - dịch vụ ăn uống tại phòng. Ví dụ: We ordered breakfast through room service. |
| business facility (noun phrase) /ˈbɪz.nɪs fəˈsɪl.ə.ti/ | amenities provided for work, like Wi-Fi or printers - tiện ích văn phòng, kinh doanh. Ví dụ: The hotel has a business facility with free internet. |
| make a request (verb phrase) /meɪk ə rɪˈkwest/ | to ask for something politely - đưa ra yêu cầu. Ví dụ: You can make a request for extra pillows. |
| file a complaint (verb phrase) /faɪl ə kəmˈpleɪnt/ | to make an official statement that something is wrong - nộp đơn khiếu nại, phản ánh. Ví dụ: The guest decided to file a complaint about the noisy AC. |
| on-the-job training (noun phrase) /ɒn ðə dʒɒb ˈtreɪ.nɪŋ/ | learning skills while doing a job - đào tạo tại chỗ. Ví dụ: The hotel provides free on-the-job training. |
| good ear (noun phrase) /ɡʊd ɪər/ | the ability to listen well and recognize sounds/accents - khả năng nghe tốt. Ví dụ: Having a good ear helps you understand accents. |
